pi_items.source: Multiple peer-reviewed orthodontic sources confirm 3mm as standard minimum safe interradicular distance for TAD placement. Source: PubMed Central, Multiple orthodontic literature reviews (2024-2025)
FDI World Dental Federation notation standard: #27 = upper left second molar; #43 = lower right canine (Wikipedia, Radiopaedia)
FDI World Dental Federation notation standard: #28 = upper left third molar; #44 = lower right first premolar (Wikipedia, multiple dental education sources)

pi_items.metadata_value: Blue Sky Plan dental planning software
pi_items.verified_value: Blue Sky Plan version 5.0 - real dental/orthodontic 3D planning software from Blue Sky Bio
pi_items.source: Official Blue Sky Bio website (blueskyplan.com, blueskybio.com). Confirmed as FDA-cleared medical device used for implant planning and surgical guide design.

privileged_info_summary: Blue Sky Plan is real FDA-cleared dental software (v5.0). The 3.0 mm safety threshold for TAD placement is clinically accurate per orthodontic literature. CRITICAL: The task description contains incorrect FDI tooth numbering—it identifies teeth as 'lower right canine (#27) and first premolar (#28)', but in FDI notation, #27 and #28 are upper left teeth (second and third molars respectively). The correct lower right teeth would be #43 (canine) and #44 (first premolar). This tooth identification error is a fundamental factual mistake in the task context.
